rationale: Goukens et al. is cited in the theory section to support a specific claim that private self-focus changes choice by increasing reliance on personal attribute weights, not just as a generic background mention. The citing paper also uses this work in its framing argument that if smartphone use elevates private self-focus, it should affect consumer choices in meaningful ways. Although the new studies examine uniqueness preferences rather than compromise/variety effects, the cited paper is part of the conceptual base used to justify the hypotheses.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Goukens et al. (2009) as prior evidence that private self-focus systematically shapes choice processes and outcomes. It builds on that foundation to propose and test a new antecedent (personal smartphone use) and a new downstream outcome (preference for unique options).
